figures.caption: Figure 4: Abstention performance across all evaluated configurations on MM-AQA (avg MCC across A-MMMU and A-MMLBD; baselines at oracle τ). Markers denote system type: circles = standalone VLM/ baseline, squares = MAS-Sequential, diamonds = MAS-Iterative. Colors denote model: green = Qwen 2.5-32B-VL, orange = GPT-5, purple = Claude Sonnet 4.5, blue = confidence and reasoning baselines, gray = degenerate anchors. The brown dashed line marks the estimated human expert oracle (MCC ≈ 0.83; Appendix J); the red bracket shows the gap (∆MCC ≈ 0.49) to the best evaluated system (VLM Claude Sonnet 4.5, avg MCC = 0.344). This confirms that MM-AQA poses a substantial and unresolved challenge for current frontier VLMs and MAS architectures.
Figure 5: Transformation Category - Missing Visual Info. Transformation type - Aggressive Multi Mask Left: Original MMMU Sample; Right: Transformed Sample
Table 6: Evidence-sufficiency classification patterns for A-MMLBD routing.

Figure 1: Overview of MM-AQA: (A) Benchmark construction: answerable instances are transformed into unanswerable counterparts along two axes, then filtered by a Dual-Consensus VLM QC module and human annotators, yielding 2079 samples. (B) Evaluation framework: standalone VLM and MAS are evaluated under a 3× 2 - condition × clause design; responses are categorised by a five-way confusion matrix and four metrics are computed. (C) Key finding: the Accuracy-Abstention trade-off across all configurations: no system simultaneously exceeds 65% on both answerable and unanswerable accuracy, revealing a miscalibration of current multimodal reasoners.

Table 1: Results on Abstain-MMMU. AAC, UAC, AR, and Best MCC (highest MCC across all abstention clause-condition variants for each system-model pair) are reported. Standard errors are computed as √ p(1 − p)/n where n is the subset size. n → AAC=300; UAC=253; AR=553.
Table 5: MM-AQA dataset statistics by subset.

Table 4: Per-transformation UAC under a fixed diagnostic configuration (standalone VLM, Claude Sonnet 4.5, ExAb, CoT), chosen to avoid ceiling compression from over-abstaining MAS setups. Withindataset comparisons are appropriate; absolute values should not be compared across benchmarks given the ∼27pp gap in baseline abstention tendencies (A-MMMU: 47.4%; A-MMLBD: 74.5%). The consistent within-dataset pattern reflects structural explicitness: categories where unanswerability is legibly encoded in input structure, broken question text, absent document pages, fall above their dataset mean UAC, while categories where evidence is present but degraded, corrupted, or contradictory fall below it. Standard errors: √ p(1 − p)/n; categories with N<100 carry higher uncertainty.

Table 2: Results on Abstain-MMLongBench-Doc. AAC, UAC, AR, and Best MCC (highest MCC across all abstention clause-condition variants for each system-model pair) are reported. Standard errors are computed as √ p(1 − p)/n where n is the subset size. n → AAC=837; UAC=689; AR=1526.
Figure 2: Taxonomy and transformation distribution for unanswerable samples across both benchmarks. Left: Abstain-MMMU; Right: Abstain-MMLongBench-Doc

Table 3: Abstention baselines (Sonnet 4.5 unless noted). MCC at oracle τ. Verb. Confidence thresholds the 1-5 score from the vconf condition; oracle τ=1 (A-MMMU) / τ=4 (A-MMLBD). CoT Abstention: single forward pass, T=0.1, standard clause. Self-Consistency: N=10, T=0.7; abstains when no answer achieves majority. P(True): self-assessed correctness probability; oracle τ=0.855 (A-MMMU) / τ=0.905 (A-MMLBD); abstains below threshold. MaxProb (oracle τ=0.98 on A-MMMU, τ=0.99 on A-MMLBD) degenerates to abstain-always at oracle τ (Appendix G.4). †Token-level logits required; unavailable for proprietary APIs. ‡Oracle τ is an upper bound on the same test set. Standard errors: √ p(1 − p)/n; n=300/837 (AAC), n=253/689 (UAC), for A-MMMU/A-MMLBD.
Figure 3: A walkthrough example of the MM-AQA pipeline, illustrated via Abstain-MMMU curation; Abstain-MMLongBench-Doc follows the same process.
